Off Calendar Sep-27-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Continued To Sep-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian McDonald appeared by zoom for Eleanor Ormanski (executor).

The court questions why it does not have a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration on file today. Mr. McDonald stated that a final report/final distribution is completed, that it was sent out to the executor for signature, that it is in the process of being mailed back and requested a two-week continuance. Status on close of estate is continued to September 27, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If said petition is not ready to go, Mr. McDonald is to prepare a petition on status of administration telling the court what's going on and why he can't close out the estate.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2022 Continued To Sep-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

This matter was set for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 4, 1999, is admitted to probate. Eleanor A. Ormanski is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for August 2, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Continued To Jul-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Continued To Jun-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian McDonald appeared by zoom for and with Eleanor Ormanski by zoom.

The Court stated that it is a little confused as to how Ms. Ormanski came into possession of the lost will and presented a series of questions. In response to the Court inquiries, Ms. Ormanski stated that Bruno went to see the Attorney and then came down to show them (Ms. Ormanski and her Husband) the will, that the will was in Louise's (Bruno's Mother) papers, that the will that is being presented is the same as the one Bruno showed her and that Bruno did not give her a copy of it. Mr. McDonald stated that he does not know if the will was always a copy, that health care workers and other people were in and out of the house, and that Mr. Canatella (drafting Attorney) is not available. Mr. McDonald also stated that he was informed that the drafting Attorney's practice was not to retain original wills, that Mr. Roselli never kept documents and that all of the documents were kept by Mr. Roselli's Mother in the Mother's room. Ms. Ormanski stated that Ed (her Husband) took Bruno to Mr. Canatella's office to get a will, that she believes it was Bruno's idea because they talked about it when the Mother got ill, that she did not got with Bruno and her Husband, and that Mr. Canatella did not do her will. Ms. Ormanski also stated that they went to Richard Canatella because he was Italian, that Mr. Canatella did the Mother's will and took care of them, that she lives in San Bruno in her home of fifty years and that she is 91 years of age. The Court stated that it needs more facts regarding the circumstances of the lost will, and that it is concerned about the circumstances of the making of the will and the ability to get this estate closed. Mr. McDonald stated that Ms. Ormanski was appointed as the Conservator of the Mother until the Mother's time of death at the end of January 2000, that she was appointed as the Conservator of Mr. Roselli, and requested that the Court take judicial notice of the conservatorship case and the Court Investigator's reports. The Court stated that the Court Investigator's reports will not touch on the issue of testamentary capacity. Mr. McDonald stated that the reports will show Mr. Roselli's medical condition and medications. The Court stated that Counsel will need to prepare a brief regarding admissibility of the lost will that is focused on whether the will can be admitted and that he is satisfied that they can bring this estate to a close. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to June 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Supplemental pleadings are to be filed by June 22, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.
